name: Scan Cone
summary: A rotating volumetric Cesium cone for searchlights, radar beams, camera coverage, drones, and alarm ranges.
useCases: Drone detection and rotating search cone

center.description: WGS84 origin point. Optional height is supported.

color.description: CSS color used by the cone material.

speed.description: Rotation and material animation speed multiplier.

showOrigin.description: Show a point marker at the cone origin.

minimal.code:
import { createScanConeEffect } from '@ztgkzhaohao/geo-effect-kit'

const cone = createScanConeEffect(viewer, {
  center: { longitude: 116.391, latitude: 39.907 },
  type: 'searchlight',
  color: '#7cf7ff',
  radiusMeters: 2200,
  lengthMeters: 6200,
  speed: 1.2,
  aperture: 38,
})

cone.flyTo()
cone.destroy()

update.code:
cone.update({
  type: 'alarm',
  color: '#ff315a',
  speed: 2.6,
  heading: 45,
  showOrigin: false,
})

notes: Pass an existing Cesium Viewer; the SDK does not create or own the viewer.
The effect uses Cesium CylinderGraphics with topRadius 0 and a custom GeoScanConeMaterial.
Changing center, radiusMeters, lengthMeters, or aperture rebuilds cone geometry.
Call destroy() during page cleanup to remove the data source and render loop.
